query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Supersed', 'Replaced by something newer or better', 'Her supersed became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Supersede', 'To replace in power, effectiveness, use, etc', 'Over time, the community learned to supersede its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),

  ('Supine', 'Lying on the back, face or front upward', 'The essay highlights the supine of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Supplant', 'To replace or take the place of someone or something, typically by force or through strategy', 'Critics argued that the plan might supplant the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Supple', 'Bending readily without breaking or becoming deformed', 'The essay highlights the supple of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),

  ('Supplicant', 'Begging, pleading, supplicating', 'Scholars noted a striking supplicant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Supplicate', 'Humble oneself before another in making a request, to beg', 'The author hoped the new policy would supplicate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Supplication', 'Humble prayer, entreaty or petition', 'The essay highlights the supplication of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Supposition', 'An uncertain belief', '“His argument was based on mere supposition.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suppress', 'Forcibly put an end to', 'The essay highlights the suppress of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suppressible', 'Capable of being done away with', 'Scholars noted a striking suppressible between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suppression', 'The act or instance of repressing', 'Her suppression became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Supramundane', 'Situated above the world or above our system, celestial', 'Scholars noted a striking supramundane between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surcharge', 'An additional charge, tax or cost', 'Her surcharge became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surety', 'Security against loss or damage, guarantee', 'The essay highlights the surety of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surfeit', 'Excessive amount', 'The essay highlights the surfeit of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surly', 'Lordly, arrogant, supercilious', 'She spoke surly, leaving little doubt about her position.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surmise', 'Guess without proof', 'The essay highlights the surmise of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surmount', 'To get over, to overcome', 'The author hoped the new policy would surmount tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surpass', 'Exceed; be greater than', 'The essay highlights the surpass of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surreptitious', 'Done secretly', '“She cast a surreptitious glance at the clock.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surrogate', 'To substitute something with something else', 'The author hoped the new policy would surrogate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surround', 'To encircle', 'Critics argued that the plan might surround the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surveillance', 'A watch kept over a person', 'Scholars noted a striking surveillance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Surveyor', 'A person whose occupation is to survey land or buildings', 'The author hoped the new policy would surveyor tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Susceptibility', 'Vulnerability', 'The essay highlights the susceptibility of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Susceptible', 'Likely or liable to be influenced or harmed by', 'The reviewer described the tone as susceptible, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suspect', 'To suppose (something) to be true without evidence', 'Over time, the community learned to suspect its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suspense', 'A state of uncertainty or excitement in awaiting an outcome', 'The essay highlights the suspense of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suspicious', 'Questionable', 'The passage presents a suspicious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sustain', 'To strengthen or support physically or mentally', 'Over time, the community learned to sustain its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sustenance', 'Something that provides support or nourishment', 'Her sustenance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Svelte', 'Attractively thin, gracefully slender', 'Her svelte became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Swarthy', 'Tawny, dusky, dark', 'The essay highlights the swarthy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Swathe', 'To bind with a swathe, band, bandage, or rollers', 'Over time, the community learned to swathe its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sway', 'To have control or influence over someone or something', 'Over time, the community learned to sway its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sybarite', 'A person devoted to luxury and pleasure', 'Critics argued that the plan might sybarite the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sycophant', 'A person who acts obsequiously to gain advantage', 'The essay highlights the sycophant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Syllabic', 'Of, relating to, or consisting of a syllable or syllables', 'Her syllabic became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Syllabication', 'The act of dividing something into syllables', 'Scholars noted a striking syllabication between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Syllable', 'Segment of speech: a vowel sound or syllabic consonant', 'The passage presents a syllable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Syllabus', 'An outline of the contents of a curriculum', 'Scholars noted a striking syllabus between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sylph', 'A wood nymph', 'Scholars noted a striking sylph between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sylvan', 'Pertaining to the forest, or woodlands', 'The author hoped the new policy would sylvan tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Symbiosis', 'Any interdependent relationship between two things', 'Her symbiosis became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Symbiotic', 'Mutually beneficial', '“Their business partnership was symbiotic.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sympathetic', 'Of, related to, showing, or characterized by sympathy', 'Scholars noted a striking sympathetic between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
